response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced software craftsman, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and web/cloud stacks built over 20+ years. C++ appears only briefly: a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a bachelor thesis project. There is no evidence of STL-heavy development, multi-threaded C++ systems, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443 compliance, or industrial automation domain knowledge — all of which are core requirements for this position.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals including TDD, CI/CD, agile, and mentoring, which align with the role's behavioral competencies.
Demonstrated experience building high-performance, high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic), showing relevant systems-thinking ability.
Event-driven architecture and REST API experience across multiple roles, partially overlapping with the message-broker and REST competencies listed.
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent at a senior level — the only meaningful exposure was an entry-level role nearly 20 years ago and a student thesis project.
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, or industrial cybersecurity background whatsoever; no familiarity with Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, or MQTT.

Entire career trajectory points away from the role — deep investment in web, TypeScript, and AI-agent tooling makes a pivot to senior C++ industrial development implausible without significant retraining.

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 exposure
Strong distributed systems and craftsmanship foundations
